Class AppliedRuleStopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.engine.impl.AppliedRuleStopCondition<Goal>
All Implemented Interfaces:
StopCondition<Goal>
public final class AppliedRuleStopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>> extends Object implements StopCondition<Goal>

Implementation of StopCondition which stops the strategy after a reached limit of rules or after a timeout in ms.

This is the default StopCondition used during verification.

  • Constructor Summary

    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull String
    getGoalNotAllowedMessage(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
    Returns the reason why the previous check via StopCondition.isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.
    int
    getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
    Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar.
    @NonNull String
    getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    boolean
    isGoalAllowed(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
    Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied.
    boolean
    shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

  • Constructor Details

    • AppliedRuleStopCondition

      public AppliedRuleStopCondition()

  • Method Details

    • getMaximalWork

      public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
      Specified by:
      getMaximalWork in interface StopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      Returns:
      The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

    • isGoalAllowed

      public boolean isGoalAllowed(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
      Specified by:
      isGoalAllowed in interface StopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the proof search (apply strategy) has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

    • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

      public @NonNull String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Returns the reason why the previous check via StopCondition.isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.
      Specified by:
      getGoalNotAllowedMessage in interface StopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      description of the reason why automatic proof search has stopped

    • shouldStop

      public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      Specified by:
      shouldStop in interface StopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

    • getStopMessage

      public @NonNull String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      Specified by:
      getStopMessage in interface StopCondition<Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      The human-readable message which explains the stop reason.
